commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

fix(config): resolve CORS_ORIGINS SettingsError on startup

pydantic-settings v2 calls json.loads() on list[str] fields before
field_validator runs, causing a SettingsError if the env var is not
valid JSON (e.g. plain URL or comma-separated string in a dashboard).

- Change CORS_ORIGINS type from list[str] to str to bypass pydantic-
  settings' complex-type JSON pre-parsing
- Replace field_validator with a @computed_field property cors_origins
  that accepts JSON arrays, comma-separated values, or a single URL
- Update main.py to use settings.cors_origins (the parsed list)
- Update .env.example to document both accepted formats

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include nation in CHPP link; sync per-team league

- Add LeagueID to each team's array in getTeamDetails so multi-team
  users carry the correct league for each team
- In updateCHPPPlayerFields, set WageWizard_League select value and
  WageWizard.LeagueDetails from the active team's LeagueID so that
  formSerialize() captures the correct nation in the generated link,
  even though the league select is hidden in CHPP mode

* test

* Populate league select before setting CHPP nation

createCountryDropbox() was only called in non-CHPP mode, leaving
the WageWizard_League select with no <option> elements in CHPP
mode. Setting .value on an empty select is a no-op, so the team's
country ID was never actually stored in the field and was missing
from the serialized link. Call createCountryDropbox() in the
CHPP OK path before setupCHPPPlayerFields so the select has
options when updateCHPPPlayerFields sets its value.

* Remove named URL params recognition

Drop fillFormFromPlayerParams and PLAYER_URL_PARAMS. The named
?goalkeeping=X&age=Y&nationality=Z parameter scheme is no longer
needed; the standard ?params= serialized format is the only
supported way to pre-fill the form from a URL.

Add a new encoding package with base62 encoding for compact, URL-safe
representations of binary data and integers.

New functions:
- Base62Encode — encode bytes to base62 string
- Base62Decode — decode base62 string to bytes
- Base62EncodeUint64 — encode uint64 to compact base62
- Base62DecodeUint64 — decode base62 string to uint64

Add ReworkContextService that assembles a validated RejectionContext from
persisted review data (LeadReviewDecision + specialist ReviewPackets +
DevResultPacket) for inclusion in rework TaskPackets.

New files:
- ports/rework-context.ports.ts — Port interfaces for querying rejected
  review data
- services/rework-context.service.ts — Service that builds RejectionContext
  at query time from already-persisted data
- services/rework-context.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering happy path,

redundantly, keeping data normalized and avoiding schema migrations.

Add functionality to enable star tracker constraints to be included in the scheduler (#27)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* feat: roll-aware visibility with star-tracker earth-limb keep-outs

- Add solar power fraction helpers and roll sweep functions to roll.py
- ScheduleProcessor sweeps candidate rolls (0-360° in roll_step increments)
  filtered by min_power_frac, selecting the roll that maximises visible time
- Roll sweep only activates when st_* constraints are explicitly set;
  vanilla ScheduleProcessor(tle1, tle2) behaves as before
- Fix atomic extend/shrink in _fix_visibility to prevent overlaps when
  remaining duration < min_sequence_duration
- Thread precomputed rolls through _fix_visibility and validate_visibility
- Update pyproject.toml deps to match pandoravisibility (notebook ^7.5.4,
  black ^26.1.0)
- Add test_gap_filling.py (18 tests) and roll sweep tests in test_roll.py
- Add roll-aware-visibility-example.ipynb demo notebook

* docs: fix solar power fraction docstrings — Y is panel hinge axis, not normal

The formula sin(arccos(|dot(Y,sun)|)) is correct and matches
pandoravisibility's get_visibility_best_roll exactly. The docstring
incorrectly stated +Y was the 'solar-panel normal' with Lambert's
cosine law. On Pandora, +Y is the panel hinge axis: power = 1.0
when Y ⊥ Sun (panels face-on), power = 0.0 when Y ∥ Sun (edge-on).

* Update solar panel power fraction threshold and add integration tests for VDA and NIRDA sequences

- Changed the minimum acceptable solar-panel power fraction from 0.8 to 0.7 in the ScheduleProcessor class.
- Added a new integration test file `test_integration_counts.py` to validate the functionality of VDA and NIRDA integration calculations.
- Implemented multiple test cases to ensure correct handling of overheads and frame/integration counts for both VDA and NIRDA sequences.

* fix: correct precomputed_rolls type hints to Dict[str, Optional[float]]

* fix: remove jupyter-contrib-nbextensions (incompatible with notebook v7, breaks mkdocs build)

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Breaking up a comment into two lines to meet line length requirements (Black)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* changing min_sequenc_duration

* bumping version

* perf: precompute Sun vectors once per times array in roll sweep

compute_mean_solar_power was called inside the candidate-roll loop
and iterated over time samples calling get_sun() per sample, giving
O(N_candidates x N_times) ephemeris lookups per target.

Fix:
- Add _sun_vectors(times) which calls get_sun() once for the whole
  times array and returns an (N, 3) float64 array via vectorised
  numpy ops (no Python loop).
- Add _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs(ra, dec, roll_deg, sun_vecs)
  which computes the mean power fraction from a precomputed sun_vecs
  array using a single batched dot-product (sun_vecs @ y_payload).
- Refactor compute_mean_solar_power to delegate to both helpers,
  removing the per-sample Python loop (public API unchanged).
- find_best_roll_for_target now precomputes sun_vecs = _sun_vectors(times)
  once before the candidate loop and calls _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs
  inside, reducing ephemeris cost from O(N_candidates x N_times) to
  O(N_times).

103 tests pass, black/flake8 clean.

* changing default post sequence to 120 s

Issue CHECKPOINT on all pool databases before SLO transitions to flush
dirty pages and minimize redo work during scaling. Includes:
- CheckpointDatabasesInPoolAsync in SqlRepository with concurrent execution
- Configurable concurrency (CheckpointConcurrency, default 5, max 25)
- Configurable post-checkpoint delay (PostCheckpointDelaySeconds, default 3)
- Best-effort: checkpoint failures never block scaling operations
- Defense-in-depth exception handling in both AutoScaler and SqlRepository
- 66 tests covering checkpoint behavior, config validation, error handling

* fix: add user secrets support for local dev, revert AppInsights to 2.23.0 for Functions v4 compatibility

* docs: update README, CLAUDE.md, and flowchart for checkpoint-before-scaling feature

* chore: add checkpoint timing script and update gitignore

- Script uses AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION env var or current az subscription
- Discovers all Hyperscale pools and measures CHECKPOINT duration
- Updated .gitignore for AzuriteConfig, beads, timing CSVs

* Update docs/plans/2026-03-05-checkpoint-before-scaling.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: validate PostCheckpointDelaySeconds against negative values

Add to existing negative-value guard for consistency with other numeric
config fields. Adds test coverage.

* fix: gitignore AzuriteConfig files (not just directories)

The trailing slash pattern only matched directories, but Azurite
creates extensionless JSON files named AzuriteConfig.

SaaS usage reporting (#2690)

* Add saas:usage_report rake task and extract Subscription.paid scope

Add a rake task to generate a CSV usage report with per-account data:
Queenbee ID, sign up date, paid date, card count, storage used, and
last active date.

Extract the paid subscriptions query from Admin::StatsController into
an Account::Subscription.paid scope so both the controller and the
new rake task can share it.

* Add comped and account name columns to usage report

* Update saas/lib/tasks/fizzy/usage_report.rake

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preload storage_total to avoid N+1 in usage report

* Batch last_active_at queries to avoid per-account aggregates

* Add tests for Account::Subscription.paid scope

* Update saas/app/models/account/subscription.rb

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Aggregate paid dates in SQL instead of loading all subscriptions

* Fix paid scope to derive plan keys from Plan.all instead of PLANS hash

* Move paid dates and comped lookups into per-batch queries

* Materialize batch IDs to avoid cross-database subquery

SaasRecord models live on a separate database (fizzy_saas) that doesn't
have the accounts table. Using batch.select(:id) generated a subquery
that ran on the saas database, causing a table-not-found error. Using
pluck(:id) materializes the IDs into an array instead.
